topic: IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scoring — Spec Consolidation
context: Two near-duplicate pipeline items (BACK-005 and BACK-006) had been sitting in the backlog with no detailed spec, while Phase 1 of the work they described had already shipped on 2026-03-30 (commit 36f9166, ic_calculator.py). This session audited both items, documented what was built, defined the remaining phases precisely, and consolidated everything into a single authoritative spec.

findings.duplicate_analysis: BACK-005 (Alphalens IC analysis) and BACK-006 (walk-forward weight calibration) were always sequential phases of one feature, not independent items. BACK-005 was the diagnostic pre-cursor; BACK-006's own documentation said 'start with IC-proportional weights before attempting full optimization.' Phase 1 IS that simpler alternative.

phase_1_already_shipped.what_it_does: Rolling 60-trade Spearman IC per dimension, weight proportional to positive IC, equal-weight fallback below 20 valid records, weekly Sunday update cycle, dashboard IC weights panel

remaining_work.phase_2: IC noise floor (0.03), HHI concentration cap (40% max), Alphalens quantile/decay/turnover analysis — needs 200+ closed trades
remaining_work.phase_3: Walk-forward HMM-conditioned per-regime IC weights — needs BACK-003 + 600+ regime-labeled trades

deliverables.description: Complete 3-phase spec: IC definition, weight derivation, data requirements, Signal object integration, success metrics, open questions, risks, references, backlog disposition

changes: BACK-005: status pending -> superseded, added superseded_by, resolution, resolved
BACK-006: status pending -> superseded, added superseded_by, resolution, resolved
BACK-011: new Phase C item — IC Phase 2 (noise floor + Alphalens), blocks BACK-003
BACK-003: dependency BACK-005 -> BACK-011 (superseded item replaced with active successor)

spec_highlights.ic_definition: Spearman rank correlation between Z-scored dimension score and 5-trading-day forward return (first close >= T+7 calendar days). Rolling 60-trade window, min 20 valid records.
spec_highlights.weight_rule_phase_1: weight_i = max(IC_i, 0); normalize to sum=1; equal-weight fallback if all IC <= 0 or insufficient data
spec_highlights.weight_rule_phase_2: weight_i = IC_i if IC_i >= 0.03 else 0; HHI cap 40% per dimension; normalize; equal-weight fallback
spec_highlights.weight_rule_phase_3: Per-regime IC vectors blended by HMM regime probabilities: sum(P(regime_r) * IC_weights_r), renormalized
spec_highlights.primary_success_metric: >=5 percentage point win rate improvement over equal-weight baseline on rolling 60 trades

open_questions_for_amit: Should IC_MIN_THRESHOLD (0.03) live in config.py['ic_calculator'] or be hard-coded in ic_calculator.py? (Recommendation: config.py for operational tunability)
Should the VIX regime router be disabled when Phase 3 HMM weights go live? (Recommendation: yes, to avoid double-counting)
Should HHI concentration cap (40% max weight) be enforced in code or only monitored in Phase 2? (Recommendation: enforce — clip and renormalize)
next_session_trigger: Pull BACK-011 (Phase 2) when trade count reaches 200 closed trades. Check /api/trade_count or alpha_gate status before starting.
